0-1-3-6-9_b9.label: genuine-ambiguity
0-1-3-6-9_b9.note: D#m7(b5,add13)/A is defensible; the set is the symmetric dim7 {C,Eb,Gb,A} plus the chromatic Db, so root assignment is inherently ambiguous and every source picks a different root (whatchord D#, music21 F#, tonal Db). It is not a functional dominant: the notes are a subset of the C half-whole octatonic but lack any 3rd and b7, so no tritone. Our #1 is a complete m7b5 with a natural 13 and the bass (b5) as a core tone, and wins a 0.10 near-tie over Cdim7(b9) via natural-extension-over-altered-color. music21's F#o7/AaddC# is enharmonically identical to our F#m6#11/A reading (same root, intervals {0,3,6,7,9}, bass A), but we rank that #3 at delta 0.22, below the near-tie cutoff, so it does not surface; among the readings the app actually shows (#1 and the #2 Cdim7(b9) near-tie) no oracle agrees. tonal roots on Db, matching nothing of ours. The intuitive bass-rooted Adim7+chromatic description ranks low because Db will not sit cleanly on Adim7. Genuine ambiguity; no engine change warranted.

The pitch set is a symmetric diminished-seventh core plus one chromatic tone, so root assignment is inherently ambiguous: music21's Fظآ»m/Cظآ» add Eظآص,C matches the surfaced Fظآ»m6ظآ»11/Cظآ» alternate, tonal's Dظآصmaj9ظآ»5sus4 gives a bass-rooted suspended major-seventh view, and Cdim7ظآص9/Dظآص is also a near-tie. 0-1-3-8_b1.label: genuine-ambiguity
0-1-3-8_b1.note: Dظآصmaj7sus2 is correct, and stronger than the b0 sibling: with Dظآص in the bass it is a complete root-position reading (all 4 notes as core template tones, bass = root, every interval consonant), so it wins on structural fit beyond the tie-break window. Ab/Db (music21, pychord) roots on the complete Ab triad but strands Dظآص as a natural 11, the avoid-note minor 9th against the major third (C); that C/Dظآص minor-9th pair is better read as root+maj7 (our Dظآص) than as two non-root color tones. tonal also re-roots away from Ab (Cm(b6,b9)/Db), so the split is triad-first matchers vs harmonic reasoners, not a true Ab consensus; ours and tonal agree on rejecting Ab as root.
